title: Light Brazilian Shaggy Bake
description: A resinous, shaggy, light Brazilian dish featuring black pepper, salt-bake, shojin-friendly. It's a unique breakfast recipe that explores the intersection of salt-baking and shojin cooking.

ingredients.name: Quinoa
ingredients.notes: Rinse and soak quinoa for 4 hours before using.

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix quinoa, sweet potatoes, coconut sugar, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
Drizzle the mixture with avocado oil and mix well.
Transfer the mixture to the prepared baking sheet and spread it evenly.
Bake for 30 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
While the mixture is baking, mix egg, ginger, lime juice, and nutritional yeast in a bowl.
Once the mixture is done, add it to the bowl with egg mixture and mix well.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
This dish is best served immediately, but it can be refrigerated for up to 24 hours and reheated in the oven before serving.
Reheat in the oven at 375°F (190°C) for 10-15 minutes or until warm and crispy on top.
This dish is a unique breakfast recipe that explores the intersection of salt-baking and shojin cooking.
It's a great option for a weeknight meal or a special occasion.
This dish is also vegan and gluten-free, making it a great option for those with dietary restrictions.
